text.title: Growth LinkedIn Strategy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Growth LinkedIn Strategy application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Posts MUST run from the operator's personal account, not the company page; corporate-only posting is rejected.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn reach for personal accounts is 5-10x higher than for company pages; B2B trust is built on faces.

rule.statement: Weekly rotation MUST include ≥3 typed posts (story / how-to / carousel); single-type repetition is rejected.
rule.rationale: Algorithm rewards type diversity; single-format streaks lose reach within 4 weeks.

rule.statement: Operator MUST reply to ≥10 strangers daily; pure broadcast accounts get suppressed.
rule.rationale: Reply-density signals 'real account' to the algorithm and drives ICP discovery via comment threads.

rule.statement: Positioning statement MUST be one ICP + one pain + one outcome; broad positioning is rejected.
rule.rationale: Generic positioning produces zero inbound; narrow positioning converts.

rule.statement: KPI set MUST track qualified DMs + inbound calls, not raw follower count.
rule.rationale: Follower count moves with bot-attracting posts; qualified DMs measure pipeline.
